data.narrative:
Proteus Space is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by David Kervin and Andrew Shapiro. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 private companies in the same sector at Series A or B stages, alongside 5 public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using $17M in total capital raised as a baseline, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $34M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector and same/adjacent-stage companies—Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), ClearSpace ($400M), and Hubble Network ($400M)—produces a median valuation of $850M, with a range spanning $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; the median P/S multiple for the sector is 64.2x across six public tickers, but Proteus Space's revenue is not on file.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $29M to $977M, with a median of $442M across the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion reflects material tension between early-stage capital-progression signals ($34M) and peer valuations skewed by later-stage funding events. Key uncertainties include whether Proteus Space's technical trajectory and market position align with the upper tier of peers (Cowboy, Aalyria) or the Series A median (ClearSpace), and the company's near-term revenue visibility relative to public-market multiples.
